prediction.review_text:
As we delve into the integration of AI tools into our business processes, it's essential to focus on the specific challenges that arise with effective AI use. At CodingRippler, we emphasize practical applications of tools like n8n and Zapier to automate workflows that enhance operational efficiency and accuracy.

For example, in a recent project, we automated data extraction from Gmail, processed it through AI, and organized it into Google Sheets. This approach not only saved our client significant manual effort but also minimized errors, allowing them to concentrate on strategic decision-making instead of repetitive tasks.

The critical takeaway is that successful AI integration requires meticulous planning and a commitment to delivering consistent, repeatable results that genuinely add value. It's not merely about adopting the latest technology; it's about ensuring it effectively addresses the challenges we face in our operations.

I encourage fellow founders and tech enthusiasts to reflect on how these tools can be thoughtfully integrated into your operations. Let's continue to share our experiences and insights to foster a community of innovation and support. If you're interested in exploring similar automation strategies or have insights to share, please reach out—I’d love to discuss potential collaborations!

actual.review_text:
AI tools aren't the edge.

The instructions are.

Everyone has access to the same AI tools now.

What makes the difference is how you instruct them.

At CodingRippler, we're heavily investing in building scenario-based instructions for our engineering team.

→ Check this before delivery
→ Flag this if it breaks
→ Don't miss this

AI runs through them. Every time.

The tool didn't change. The way we use it did.

Faster delivery. Consistent quality.

If you've been waiting to build because "agencies cost too much" that's changed.

 

#AI #Startup #Founder #Entrepreneur #SmallBusiness #Agency

In today's fast-paced tech environment, effective collaboration is essential for project success. As the founder of CodingRippler, I've witnessed the remarkable dedication and expertise of Indian technical talent. Our team specializes in automation, orchestration, and AI applications, consistently delivering reliable and repeatable solutions tailored for startups and SMBs.

Utilizing tools like n8n, Make, and Zapier, we empower founders to streamline operations and achieve their goals efficiently. Our focus extends beyond product delivery; we prioritize building partnerships that foster innovation and growth.

The strong work ethic and problem-solving skills of Indian developers enable us to create impactful solutions. By embracing collaboration, we leverage diverse perspectives that lead to more effective outcomes. If you're looking to enhance your project with exceptional technical talent, let's connect! Together, we can drive meaningful change in the tech industry and unlock the potential of dedicated technical partnerships, all while embracing the importance of resilience and adaptability in our collaborative efforts.

actual.review_text:
I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

"Do you guys work 24/7? I sent that bug report at 1 PM CST and it was fixed before my 4 PM meeting. That's midnight in India!"

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

Working with Indian developers feels different. There's this hunger to solve problems no matter how complex they get.

But most international founders miss this:

It's not about finding ANY Indian developer. It's about finding the RIGHT technical implementation partner who bridges your vision with flawless execution.

That's exactly what CodingRippler is built for.

We're the technical implementation partner for this era. Matching world-class Indian talent to your specific business needs. Whether it's n8n automation, API integrations, or complex system builds.

Ready to discuss your technical needs? DM me - I personally handle every partnership See our client results:[

#TechnicalPartnership #StartupFounders #n8nAutomation #APIIntegration #TechResources #IndianDevelopers #TechnicalTeam #AutomationExperts #BacklogClearing #TechImplementation #ScaleUp #TechPartner #QualityDelivery #CodingRippler

actual.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

prediction.review_text:
Navigating the job market as a software engineer or product manager can be daunting, especially in today's fast-paced tech landscape. I've been reflecting on the importance of clear and effective communication during this journey. It's not just about having the right skills; it's about how we present ourselves and our experiences. 

Here are a few actionable tips that I've found helpful:

1. **Tailor Your Resume**: Highlight the skills and experiences that are most relevant to the position you're applying for. Use keywords from the job description to ensure your resume stands out.

2. **Network Authentically**: Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, or even connections on LinkedIn. Share your journey and seek advice. Authentic connections often lead to unexpected opportunities.

3. **Prepare for Interviews**: Practice articulating your experiences and how they relate to the role. Use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) to structure your responses.

4. **Showcase Your Projects**: Whether it's a GitHub repository or a personal blog, having a portfolio of your work can demonstrate your skills and passion to potential employers.

5. **Stay Updated**: The tech industry evolves rapidly. Engage with online courses, webinars, and community discussions to keep your skills sharp and relevant.

Remember, your journey is unique, and it's okay to seek support along the way. Let's uplift each other in our career transitions and celebrate our wins, no matter how small. Together, we can navigate this landscape with confidence and resilience. #CareerDevelopment #JobMarket #SoftwareEngineering #ProductManagement #CommunitySupport

As we navigate the ever-evolving landscape of technology, it's crucial to emphasize the role of programming education in shaping our future. 🎓✨ In my journey as a software engineer at Microsoft, I've witnessed firsthand the importance of equipping the next generation with essential skills for a tech-driven world. Through hands-on coding bootcamps, online courses, and community workshops, we have a responsibility to make programming accessible to everyone, regardless of their background. I'm particularly proud to support initiatives that promote tech education, especially within the Armenian community. Organizations like the Armenian Code Academy (ACA) and COAF SMART are empowering young minds with the knowledge and tools to succeed. 

While advocating for accessibility in programming, we must also recognize the emotional aspects of career transitions. Resilience and adaptability are vital, and a supportive community can make all the difference during these challenging times. Եթե ուզում եք ծրագրավորում սովորել, կարող եք դիմել rAcademy՝ հերթական դասընթացին մասնակցելու համար։

Առաջին փուլը իր մեջ է ներառում երեք դասընթացներ՝

- Digital thinking
- Discrete Math
- Software development essentials with Java

Երկրորդ փուլի ընթացքում կուսումնասիրենք հետևյալ առարկաները՝

- Data Structures
- Algorithms
- Data Engineering

Դասընթացները տևելու են 3-4 ամիս՝ շաբաթական 3 անգամյա օնլայն հանդիպումներով ։ Դասավանդողների կողմից կիրականացվի հարցազրույց՝ դասընթացներում ընդգրկվելու համար։

Կա սիմվոլիկ վճար, որը ամբողջությամբ վերադարձվելու է լավ առաջադիմություն ցուցաբերած ուսանողներին։

Բոլոր մանրամասները և դիմելու կարգը տեսեք ֆեյսբուքի այս էջում․

actual.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

actual.review_text:
I sat down with The AI Kubernetes Show at KubeCon Atlanta 2025 to discuss the biggest challenges AI is bringing to our platforms.

In this limited podcast series by @Buoyant, I chatted with @William Chia about the challenges of bridging the divide between platform engineering and data science. Listen to the full episode now, and let me know what you think in the comments section. 
 

Be sure to check out Chris Khanoyan and Ahmed Bebars's episode, which went live today as well. And look forward to Dinesh Majrekar, Grant Miller, and Ramiro Berrelleza podcasts coming up next week. 

#KubeCon #Kubernetes #AI #PlatformEngineering #KubernetesAIShow

actual.review_text:
Attention all friends and fellow engineers! I'm reaching out to you today because I'm in dire need of your help. Unfortunately, some truly exceptional engineers were let go from our team this week. These colleagues have been instrumental in my growth and development within the company, and I've learned so much from them

That's why I'm reaching out to you, my trusted network. I was hoping that perhaps someone out there might be able to offer a referral for these amazing engineers. They are not only incredibly talented, but also wonderful individuals to work with. They have a passion for their craft that is unmatched, and their expertise has made a significant impact on our team.

So, if you or someone you know is looking to hire talented and driven engineers, please don't hesitate to reach out. I would be forever grateful for your help in finding a new home for these amazing individuals. Together, we can help them land on their feet and continue to thrive in their careers. Thank you in advance for your help!

Some additional info:
- These engineers are based in Vancouver or in the US
- Focused more on backend development though have full stack experience

Edit: Thanks for all the help everyone! Please keep them coming!

5b792818-ac34-4256-97f8-cdce43bde7f0.product_description: Support and effectiveness of caregiver leave in a workplace

prediction.review_text:
As a software engineer navigating the fast-paced world of tech, I've come to appreciate the profound impact that caregiver leave can have on our lives and careers. Recently, I took some time off to support a family member, and it was a transformative experience that reinforced the importance of mental health and resilience in our industry. 

In the tech world, where we often prioritize deadlines and deliverables, it’s crucial to recognize that our personal lives and responsibilities shape who we are as professionals. Companies that offer robust caregiver leave policies not only support their employees but also foster a culture of empathy and understanding. This, in turn, leads to a more engaged and productive workforce.

I encourage my peers and leaders in tech to advocate for policies that prioritize the well-being of our teams. Let’s create environments where taking time for family is seen as a strength, not a setback. Together, we can build a more inclusive and supportive workplace that values both professional achievements and personal journeys. 

How has your experience been with caregiver leave? Let’s share our stories and support one another in this journey.

prediction.review_text: As I reflect on my career journey, I am deeply grateful for the transitions that have shaped my professional path. From my early days at Adobe to my current role at Microsoft, each experience has imparted invaluable lessons about resilience and adaptability, particularly in overcoming impostor syndrome. Navigating through various companies and roles has not only challenged me technically but has also highlighted the critical importance of community support and mentorship. Sharing our experiences—both successes and challenges—fosters a culture of growth and collaboration essential in our industry. In the ever-evolving landscape of tech, especially in AI and data science, embracing change is vital. I encourage my peers to view career transitions not just as challenges, but as profound opportunities for personal and professional development. Let’s uplift one another in our journeys, celebrate our milestones, and advocate for mental health and well-being in our workplaces. Together, we can cultivate an inclusive environment that empowers everyone to thrive. What lessons have your career transitions taught you? I’d love to hear your stories and insights in the comments! #CareerDevelopment #Resilience #Mentorship #CommunitySupport #AI #DataScience

actual.review_text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe! Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe

actual.review_text:
I'm happy to present to open-source enthusiasts and computer science students about the intricacies of OpenTelemetry and how it can be leveraged to build efficient and scalable systems.

I'll also showcase how telemetry data can be fed into a data visualization tool like Grafana Labs' Grafana with the help of the `grafana-lgtm` docker container stack.

Thank you for this opportunity, Răzvan Deaconescu and ROSEdu.
Thank you to everyone who contributes to the open-source space.

actual.review_text:
This post is dedicated to my past employer, Ubisoft, and to my time spent working as a QA/QC engineer for the `Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora` video game.

I am thrilled to witness the release of the game and the positive reviews that have come along. Avatar is a vast franchise, spanning many fields of creativity.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

#Ubisoft #Avatar

Working at Ubisoft, I had the privilege of collaborating with talented teams, tackling complex challenges, and witnessing firsthand the power of creativity and technology in gaming.

One key takeaway from my time in game development is the importance of mentorship. I was fortunate to have mentors who guided me through the intricacies of game mechanics and coding practices, helping me grow both technically and personally. Their support not only enhanced my skills but also instilled in me the value of giving back. Now, I strive to pay it forward by mentoring aspiring developers, sharing insights, and fostering a supportive community.

As we navigate our careers, let’s remember to embrace our experiences, learn from our challenges, and uplift one another. The skills we acquire in one industry can often translate into unexpected strengths in another. Let’s keep pushing boundaries and supporting each other on this journey! #Mentorship #CareerDevelopment #GameDevelopment #CommunitySupport

prediction.review_text:
In today's fast-paced tech landscape, continuous professional development is not just a luxury—it's a necessity. As someone who has navigated the challenges of career growth in software engineering, I've learned that effective learning strategies can make all the difference. Here are a few actionable tips that have worked for me and many of my peers, especially during transitions:

1. **Set Clear Goals**: Define what you want to achieve in your career. Whether it's mastering a new technology, transitioning to a leadership role, or enhancing your soft skills, having a clear vision helps you stay focused.

2. **Leverage Online Resources**: Platforms like Coursera, Udacity, and even GitHub offer a wealth of knowledge. Don't hesitate to dive into tutorials, courses, or open-source projects that align with your goals.

3. **Engage with the Community**: Networking is crucial. Join tech meetups, webinars, or online forums. Sharing experiences and learning from others can provide insights that you won't find in textbooks. Remember, we grow stronger together!

4. **Seek Mentorship**: Finding a mentor can be a game-changer. They can provide guidance, support, and valuable feedback as you navigate your career path. Don't underestimate the power of shared experiences.

5. **Reflect and Adapt**: Regularly assess your progress and be open to adjusting your strategies. The tech industry evolves quickly, and so should your approach to learning. Embrace resilience and adaptability as you face challenges.

Remember, professional development is a journey, not a destination. Let's support each other in our growth, acknowledge our emotional experiences, and celebrate our milestones together! What strategies have you found effective in your career development? Share your thoughts below! #CareerDevelopment #LearningStrategies #Mentorship #CommunitySupport #Resilience

actual.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.

In today's fast-paced tech landscape, continuous professional development is not just a luxury—it's a necessity. Navigating career transitions in software engineering can be challenging, and recognizing the emotional and psychological aspects of this journey is crucial. Here are a few actionable tips that have helped me and many of my peers during these transitions:

1. **Set Clear Goals**: Define your career aspirations. Whether it's mastering a new technology, moving into a leadership role, or enhancing your soft skills, having a clear vision keeps you focused and motivated.

2. **Leverage Online Resources**: Utilize platforms like Coursera, Udacity, and GitHub to access a wealth of knowledge. Engage with tutorials, courses, or open-source projects that align with your goals, and remember to seek help from the community. We are stronger together!

3. **Engage with the Community**: Networking is crucial for growth. Attend tech meetups, webinars, or join online forums. Sharing experiences and learning from others can provide insights that textbooks often miss. Community support is vital in our journeys, especially during challenging times.

4. **Embrace Resilience and Adaptability**: Regularly assess your progress and be open to adjusting your strategies. The tech industry evolves rapidly, and so should your approach to learning. Acknowledge the emotional aspects of career transitions as they play a significant role in our growth and mental health.

5. **Value Mentorship**: Seek mentors who can guide you through your career path. Their insights and experiences can be invaluable, especially during challenging transitions. Mentorship fosters personal growth and resilience, helping you navigate the complexities of your career.

6. **Celebrate Milestones Together**: Acknowledge your achievements and those of your peers. Supporting each other reinforces the importance of community and shared experiences, making our journeys more fulfilling.

What strategies have you found effective in your career development? Let's discuss and learn from one another! #CareerDevelopment #LearningStrategies #CommunitySupport #Resilience #Mentorship #EmotionalGrowth

actual.review_text: HDFC Bank Your representatives call multiple times a week asking me if I want to increase my credit limit . If I tell them to not call me again about it , they just ignore it. Called me twice today about it. Why are you pestering people about it? Might as well get rid of HDFC credit cards & account due to the insane amount of marketing calls. None of them seem to be able to mark a customer as "not interested" and not call again. Really disappointed with you. I will also complain to banking ombudsman and to Dept of Telecom (DOT) for misusing our contact number to spam us with marketing calls. Shameful

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8.product_description: Opportunities and job searching in the tech industry

prediction.review_text:
Navigating the tech job market can feel daunting, especially with the rapid changes in our industry. As someone who has been on both sides of the hiring table, I want to share some insights that might help you in your job search. 

1. **Leverage Your Network**: Don’t underestimate the power of connections. Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, and even classmates. They can often provide leads or referrals that are not advertised.

2. **Showcase Your Skills**: In a competitive market, having a strong portfolio or GitHub profile can set you apart. Share your projects, contributions to open-source, or any relevant work that demonstrates your expertise.

3. **Stay Updated**: The tech landscape is always evolving. Make it a habit to learn about new tools and technologies, especially those relevant to your field. Certifications can also add value to your resume.

4. **Be Open to Opportunities**: Sometimes the right role might not be what you initially envisioned. Be open to exploring positions that may stretch your skills or take you into new areas of interest.

5. **Prioritize Mental Health**: Job searching can be stressful. Remember to take breaks, seek support from your community, and practice self-care. Resilience is key during this journey.

Lastly, if you're looking for opportunities or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey. Together, we can navigate the challenges and celebrate the wins! #CareerDevelopment #JobSearch #TechIndustry #CommunitySupport

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

prediction.review_text:
As someone who has navigated the complexities of pursuing higher education in the United States, I understand how overwhelming the process can be. From selecting the right program to understanding the nuances of international student requirements, it’s crucial to equip yourself with the right information and resources. 

Here are a few key pieces of advice I wish I had known earlier:

1. **Research Thoroughly**: Take the time to explore various institutions and their programs. Look beyond rankings; consider factors like faculty expertise, campus culture, and support services for international students.

2. **Connect with Alumni**: Reach out to alumni from your desired programs. They can provide invaluable insights into their experiences and offer guidance on what to expect.

3. **Understand Financial Aid Options**: Many institutions offer scholarships and financial aid specifically for international students. Make sure to investigate these opportunities early in your application process.

4. **Prepare for Cultural Adjustments**: Moving to a new country comes with its own set of challenges. Be open to learning and adapting to different cultural norms, and don’t hesitate to seek support from campus resources.

5. **Leverage Networking Opportunities**: Attend workshops, seminars, and events related to your field of study. Networking can open doors to internships and job opportunities that are crucial for your career.

Remember, pursuing higher education is not just about academics; it’s about personal growth and building a future. If you have any questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship

actual.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

Here are a few key pieces of advice I wish I had known earlier:

1. **Research Thoroughly**: Take the time to explore various institutions and their programs. Look beyond rankings; consider factors like faculty expertise, campus culture, and support services for international students.

2. **Connect with Alumni**: Reach out to alumni from your desired programs. They can provide invaluable insights into their experiences and offer guidance on what to expect.

3. **Understand Financial Aid Options**: Many institutions offer scholarships and financial aid specifically for international students. Make sure to investigate these opportunities early in your application process.

4. **Prepare for Cultural Adjustments**: Moving to a new country comes with its own set of challenges. Be open to learning and adapting to different cultural norms, and don’t hesitate to seek support from campus resources.

5. **Leverage Networking Opportunities**: Attend workshops, seminars, and events related to your field of study. Networking can open doors to internships and job opportunities that are crucial for your career.

Remember, pursuing higher education is not just about academics; it’s about personal growth and building a future. If you have any questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship

prediction.review_text:
Navigating personal challenges in our careers, especially in the tech industry, can often feel like an uphill battle. Many of us encounter obstacles such as self-doubt and the struggle to balance work with personal responsibilities. Sharing these experiences is essential to foster a supportive community that values emotional well-being alongside technical skills.

Acknowledging the emotional toll our careers can take is crucial. Mental health significantly impacts our professional lives, and cultivating resilience and adaptability is key to navigating our paths effectively. This is particularly relevant when addressing issues like Imposter Syndrome, which can hinder our growth and confidence.

I encourage everyone to share their stories related to mental health and self-doubt. Let's create a culture of support and mentorship where it's okay to ask for help and lean on our networks. Together, we can build an inclusive environment that emphasizes community support and resilience in our professional journeys, especially during challenging times like layoffs.

What challenges have you faced in your career regarding mental health and self-doubt? How have you navigated these hurdles? Let’s share our experiences and support each other, highlighting the importance of community and mentorship in overcoming these challenges. Remember, prioritizing our mental health is not just beneficial; it's essential for our productivity and growth.

actual.review_text:
Estatisticas mundias estimam que "aprox. 70% das pessoas irão vivenciar sindrome de impostor pelo menos uma vez na vida" ( Essa experiência afeta principalmente as mulheres e, caso não identificada e persistente, pode ter impacto considerável em nossa carreira. 
A comunidade Brasil do The WIT Network faz um convite a você para participar de um bate papo online sobre o tema. Venha conhecer mais sobre a Sindrome de Impostor, bem como ouvir as estorias e dicas de profissionais talentosos que vivenciaram e superaram essa experiencia. 
Data: 16 de Outubro, às 19h BRT. 

[English]
World statistics estimate that "around 70% of people in the world will experience Imposter Syndrome at least once in their lives"( It is likely to affect women. 
Non identified cases or persistently experiencing Imposter Syndrome may affect your career progression. With this in mind, the Brazilian Community of The WIT Network invites you to join an online roundtable about the subject. Join us to know more about Imposter Syndrome and hear the amazing stories from talented professionals that overcame it.
Date: October 16th at 7PM BRT (GMT-3) 
Note: the event will be held in Portuguese.

In the last week I decided to close a cycle in my life. I finished a fantastic journey of 15 years working on research and development at Eldorado. 
Thank you Eldorado friends for all support and great work that has changed so many customers' business, that has contributed to show that it is possible to do innovation and research in Brazil.
Thank you also to all partners and customers that gave me the chance to learn with you.
Now, it is time to start a new journey full of new innovation challenges. 
 Let's empower every people to innovate.
#newjourney #gratefulness #innovation

actual.review_text:
If you are interested in Data Engineering and you are based on LATAM, please read this!

At Factored we are on a mission to make a positive contribution to LATAM. The way we want to do this is by building high-caliber data teams ready to show the world the talent we have in Latin America.

Last year I embarked on a mission to help people understand more about Data Engineering. I had the opportunity to talk to almost 30 people about data engineering and share some insights about the profession with them.

I've decided again to open some slots on my schedule this year! :)
So, if you are into Data Engineering and you are interested to join Factored or you want to get some guidance on Data Engineering, please reach out to me. Also, if you know anyone interested in this topic, please tag them so we can get in touch! :)

You can find the link to grab a slot on my calendar in the comments :)
#factored #dataengineering #data #latinamerica

actual.review_text:
Good Day Friends, 
I would like to share my understanding of "COGS Split" in SAP S/4 HANA 1809 in a quick presentation.

This concept has been since 'SFin 1.0' as its one of much requested features from SAP customers.

Refer Note 2399030 (Splitting the costs of goods sold in S/4HANA)

Hope you will find it useful to some extent. Thank you. 

 #sapfico #hana #COGSSplit #S/4HANAFinance1809 #sap

dc0b5d74-87d9-4fe3-8883-aef9d0924114.product_description: SAP S4HANA system activation and basic process understanding

prediction.review_text:
🚀 I'm excited to share practical insights on activating your SAP S/4HANA system and understanding its foundational processes! 🌟 In the realm of enterprise finance, having a solid grasp of your SAP environment is essential. Here are some actionable steps to kickstart your journey:

1️⃣ **System Activation**: Access SAP CAL (Cloud Appliance Library) for a smooth activation experience. This platform allows you to explore functionalities without the complexities of a full implementation.

2️⃣ **Basic Navigation**: Familiarize yourself with the Fiori Launchpad. It enhances user experience and streamlines workflow. Utilize role-based access to customize your dashboard effectively.

3️⃣ **Understanding Key Processes**: Focus on essential modules like Finance and Controlling (FICO). Start with COGS splits and Account-Based COPA to comprehend how financial data flows through the system. This foundational knowledge will empower you to make informed decisions and optimizations.

4️⃣ **Resources for Learning**: Leverage community resources! Check out the official SAP documentation and engage with forums for real-world scenarios and solutions. This aligns with our commitment to accessibility and community support.

5️⃣ **Hands-On Practice**: The best way to learn is by doing. Set up test scenarios to explore various functionalities and configurations. This practical approach reinforces your understanding and prepares you for real-world challenges.

Mastering SAP S/4HANA is an ongoing journey, and each step enhances your expertise. Let’s keep the dialogue open! What challenges have you encountered in your SAP journey? Share your experiences below! 👇

#SAP #S4HANA #Finance #EnterpriseSolutions #LearningTogether #CommunitySupport

actual.review_text:
Are you new to #SAP #S4HANA?

I strongly recommend you do the below:

1) Activate the S4HANA 1809 free test system from SAP CAL. (I've published a video on how to do this on my YouTube channel)

2) Follow this very simple PDF to run some basic processes in Finance, Procurement, and Sales.

This will help you get a general understanding of how the system works

As you see this is a public PDF created by SAP, so please feel free to download and share it.

#AGALAL #Share_Knowledge

1️⃣ **System Activation**: Access SAP CAL (Cloud Appliance Library) for a smooth activation experience. This platform allows you to explore functionalities without the complexities of a full implementation.

2️⃣ **Basic Navigation**: Get acquainted with the Fiori Launchpad. It enhances user experience and streamlines workflow. Utilize role-based access to customize your dashboard effectively.

3️⃣ **Understanding Key Processes**: Focus on essential modules like Finance and Controlling (FICO). Begin with COGS splits and Account-Based COPA to understand how financial data flows through the system. This foundational knowledge will empower you to make informed decisions and optimizations.

4️⃣ **Resources for Learning**: Tap into community resources! Check out the official SAP documentation and engage with forums for real-world scenarios and solutions. This aligns with our commitment to accessibility and community support.

Just finished the course “Learning Django”!

Implemented models, enabled migrations, built URL handlers and URL patterns, views and templates to create a Django web application that manages Pet Adoption.

Also touched on essential practices like regular expressions and incorporating CSS and JS into webapp.

Big shout out to course author: Caleb Smith

“The battle for #artificialintelligence supremacy is a binary game between US & China ”— this is what the media likes you to believe. 

My last talk in Berlin debunked exactly this propagated myth. 

I had highlighted that AI innovation can and will come from anywhere since:
 1) talent, 
2)Infrastructure and 
3) AI tech & tools 
are accessible to anyone with internet and hunger to solve a problem. 

Do you agree?

Not many of you may know but I spent a lot of my career in the pathology laboratories doing software engineering.

I have often thought how AI will revolutionize patient care, especially in terms of diagnostic services such as radiology and pathology. So it was exciting to see new research from my current employer Microsoft and my former, the UW, rolling research into exactly this, using AI to improve cancer outcomes.

Thinking of the good old days when Rodney Schmidt and I were trying "change the world" with software!

actual.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

actual_review_data.review_text:
I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

"Do you guys work 24/7? I sent that bug report at 1 PM CST and it was fixed before my 4 PM meeting. That's midnight in India!"

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

Working with Indian developers feels different. There's this hunger to solve problems no matter how complex they get.

But most international founders miss this:

It's not about finding ANY Indian developer. It's about finding the RIGHT technical implementation partner who bridges your vision with flawless execution.

That's exactly what CodingRippler is built for.

We're the technical implementation partner for this era. Matching world-class Indian talent to your specific business needs. Whether it's n8n automation, API integrations, or complex system builds.

Ready to discuss your technical needs? DM me - I personally handle every partnership See our client results:[

#TechnicalPartnership #StartupFounders #n8nAutomation #APIIntegration #TechResources #IndianDevelopers #TechnicalTeam #AutomationExperts #BacklogClearing #TechImplementation #ScaleUp #TechPartner #QualityDelivery #CodingRippler

313325f0-0334-45f7-93b3-e65adcc597e9_rev.product_description: Webinars exploring hardware support for artificial intelligence

actual_review_data.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

351405ab-deb1-45b5-810c-d32798c95f40_rev.product_description: Job market communication for software engineers and product managers

actual_review_data.review_text: Anyone looking for a sr./principal level software engineer, sr. level product/program manager (either greater Seattle area or remote)? With the market still being crazy-competitive, some of my friends are still looking for a job after the big lay-off wave. Please ping me and I'll share their resume/profile.
actual_review_data.product_description: Job market communication for software engineers and product managers

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating the job market as a software engineer or product manager can be daunting, especially in today's fast-paced tech landscape. I've been reflecting on the importance of clear and effective communication during this journey. It's not just about having the right skills; it's about how we present ourselves and our experiences. 

Here are a few actionable tips that I've found helpful:

1. **Tailor Your Resume**: Highlight the skills and experiences that are most relevant to the position you're applying for. Use keywords from the job description to ensure your resume stands out.

2. **Network Authentically**: Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, or even connections on LinkedIn. Share your journey and seek advice. Authentic connections often lead to unexpected opportunities.

3. **Prepare for Interviews**: Practice articulating your experiences and how they relate to the role. Use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) to structure your responses.

4. **Showcase Your Projects**: Whether it's a GitHub repository or a personal blog, having a portfolio of your work can demonstrate your skills and passion to potential employers.

5. **Stay Updated**: The tech industry evolves rapidly. Engage with online courses, webinars, and community discussions to keep your skills sharp and relevant.

Remember, your journey is unique, and it's okay to seek support along the way. Let's uplift each other in our career transitions and celebrate our wins, no matter how small. Together, we can navigate this landscape with confidence and resilience. #CareerDevelopment #JobMarket #SoftwareEngineering #ProductManagement #CommunitySupport

Navigating the job market as a software engineer or product manager can be daunting, especially in today's fast-paced tech landscape. Եթե ուզում եք ծրագրավորում սովորել, կարող եք դիմել rAcademy՝ հերթական դասընթացին մասնակցելու համար։

Առաջին փուլը իր մեջ է ներառում երեք դասընթացներ՝

- Digital thinking
- Discrete Math
- Software development essentials with Java

Երկրորդ փուլի ընթացքում կուսումնասիրենք հետևյալ առարկաները՝

- Data Structures
- Algorithms
- Data Engineering

Դասընթացները տևելու են 3-4 ամիս՝ շաբաթական 3 անգամյա օնլայն հանդիպումներով ։ Դասավանդողների կողմից կիրականացվի հարցազրույց՝ դասընթացներում ընդգրկվելու համար։

Կա սիմվոլիկ վճար, որը ամբողջությամբ վերադարձվելու է լավ առաջադիմություն ցուցաբերած ուսանողներին։

Բոլոր մանրամասները և դիմելու կարգը տեսեք ֆեյսբուքի այս էջում․

As we continue to navigate the ever-evolving landscape of technology, I want to take a moment to reflect on the importance of programming education and how it can shape our future. 🎓✨ 

In my journey as a software engineer at Microsoft, I've witnessed firsthand how crucial it is to equip the next generation with the skills they need to thrive in a tech-driven world. Whether it's through hands-on coding bootcamps, online courses, or community workshops, we have a responsibility to make programming accessible to everyone, regardless of their background. 

I’m proud to be part of initiatives that promote tech education, especially within the Armenian community. Organizations like the Armenian Code Academy (ACA) and COAF SMART are doing incredible work to empower young minds with the knowledge and tools to succeed. 

Let’s continue to support these efforts and share resources that can help others on their learning journey. Together, we can foster a culture of inclusivity and innovation in technology. 

What resources or programs have you found helpful in your own learning or teaching experiences? Let’s share and grow together! 💡🤝

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
🚀 Excited to share a new series focused on introducing programming concepts and exercises! Whether you're a beginner or looking to brush up on your skills, this content is designed to make programming accessible and engaging for everyone. 

We'll cover essential topics like binary numbers, functions, and control flow, with practical exercises that you can try out right away. My goal is to demystify programming and show how these foundational concepts can empower you in your tech journey. 

I believe that everyone should have the opportunity to learn and grow in tech, and I’m committed to providing resources that support that mission. 💡 Let's build a community of learners together! 

Stay tuned for the first post in this series, and feel free to reach out if you have any specific topics you'd like to see covered. Together, we can make programming a little less intimidating and a lot more fun! #ProgrammingWithArsen #TechEducation #CommunitySupport

actual_review_data.review_text:
#programmingWithArsen

I know the first topics seem boring, so I decided to do some exercises for several reasons:

1. show that the "boring theory" is important
2. show that we still need more "boring theory" before we can do something exciting
3. make sure you understand how the things I tell about are used in the code

This week will be all about problem solving, or trying to solve at least.

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we navigate the ever-evolving landscape of AI and technology, it's fascinating to reflect on how scientific experiments and theories throughout history have shaped our current understanding and applications. From the early days of computing to the sophisticated AI models we leverage today, each breakthrough has paved the way for innovation in ways we often take for granted. 

For instance, consider how the principles of machine learning echo the foundational theories of statistics and probability. These historical insights not only inform our technical approaches but also remind us of the importance of interdisciplinary thinking in technology development. 

In my journey as a software engineer, I've seen firsthand how integrating lessons from the past can enhance our present solutions, especially in cloud security and DevOps. By applying these historical frameworks, we can create more robust, secure, and efficient systems that not only meet today's demands but are also prepared for future challenges. 

Let’s continue to draw inspiration from the past as we build the future of technology. What historical theories or experiments have influenced your work in tech? I’d love to hear your thoughts!

actual_review_data.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

actual_review_data.product_description: Challenges in enterprise AI implementations

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we continue to explore the integration of AI in enterprise environments, it's crucial to address the challenges that come with these implementations. From my experience at Microsoft, I've seen firsthand how the complexity of existing systems can hinder the seamless adoption of AI technologies. One of the key challenges is ensuring that our AI models are not only effective but also align with the operational realities of our infrastructure. 

To tackle this, we must prioritize collaboration across teams—bringing together data scientists, engineers, and business stakeholders to create a shared understanding of goals and limitations. Additionally, focusing on practical applications and real-world scenarios can help demystify AI for broader teams, making it more accessible and actionable. 

I encourage everyone in our community to share their experiences and strategies for overcoming these hurdles. Let's leverage our collective knowledge to drive innovation and ensure that AI truly enhances our enterprise capabilities. Together, we can create a more inclusive and effective approach to AI integration that benefits all stakeholders involved.

actual_review_data.review_text:
I sat down with The AI Kubernetes Show at KubeCon Atlanta 2025 to discuss the biggest challenges AI is bringing to our platforms.

In this limited podcast series by @Buoyant, I chatted with @William Chia about the challenges of bridging the divide between platform engineering and data science. Listen to the full episode now, and let me know what you think in the comments section. 
 

Be sure to check out Chris Khanoyan and Ahmed Bebars's episode, which went live today as well. And look forward to Dinesh Majrekar, Grant Miller, and Ramiro Berrelleza podcasts coming up next week. 

#KubeCon #Kubernetes #AI #PlatformEngineering #KubernetesAIShow

54c19b05-7985-47be-b2f2-6a2f8360cf89_rev.product_description: Referrals and support for talented engineers seeking new opportunities

actual_review_data.review_text:
Attention all friends and fellow engineers! I'm reaching out to you today because I'm in dire need of your help. Unfortunately, some truly exceptional engineers were let go from our team this week. These colleagues have been instrumental in my growth and development within the company, and I've learned so much from them

That's why I'm reaching out to you, my trusted network. I was hoping that perhaps someone out there might be able to offer a referral for these amazing engineers. They are not only incredibly talented, but also wonderful individuals to work with. They have a passion for their craft that is unmatched, and their expertise has made a significant impact on our team.

So, if you or someone you know is looking to hire talented and driven engineers, please don't hesitate to reach out. I would be forever grateful for your help in finding a new home for these amazing individuals. Together, we can help them land on their feet and continue to thrive in their careers. Thank you in advance for your help!

Some additional info:
- These engineers are based in Vancouver or in the US
- Focused more on backend development though have full stack experience

Edit: Thanks for all the help everyone! Please keep them coming!

🌟 Exciting times for talented engineers in our community! 🌟 

As we navigate the ever-evolving tech landscape, I want to take a moment to highlight the importance of supporting each other, especially for those seeking new opportunities. If you know any skilled engineers who are currently looking for their next role, please don't hesitate to reach out! 

I’m more than happy to provide referrals and connect them with opportunities within my network. Together, we can help bridge the gap for those who may be feeling the pressure of career transitions. 

Let’s leverage our collective strength to uplift one another! 💪 If you’re an engineer looking for new challenges or know someone who is, drop a comment or send me a message. Let’s make sure no one feels alone in this journey. 

#CareerSupport #Referrals #TechCommunity #Engineers #Networking

5b792818-ac34-4256-97f8-cdce43bde7f0_rev.product_description: Support and effectiveness of caregiver leave in a workplace

actual_review_data.review_text: Cannot agree more, the last two weeks of caregiver leave I received was super helpful for me to take care of my father. Over and above the immense support from my manager & LT during this time ensured that despite this being unplanned I did not receive a single call/ping from business & my team! I was able to focus on my father's health only. #microsoftlife #microsoftculture
actual_review_data.product_description: Support and effectiveness of caregiver leave in a workplace

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As a software engineer navigating the fast-paced world of tech, I've come to appreciate the profound impact that caregiver leave can have on our lives and careers. Recently, I took some time off to support a family member, and it was a transformative experience that reinforced the importance of mental health and resilience in our industry. 

In the tech world, where we often prioritize deadlines and deliverables, it’s crucial to recognize that our personal lives and responsibilities shape who we are as professionals. Companies that offer robust caregiver leave policies not only support their employees but also foster a culture of empathy and understanding. This, in turn, leads to a more engaged and productive workforce.

I encourage my peers and leaders in tech to advocate for policies that prioritize the well-being of our teams. Let’s create environments where taking time for family is seen as a strength, not a setback. Together, we can build a more inclusive and supportive workplace that values both professional achievements and personal journeys. 

How has your experience been with caregiver leave? Let’s share our stories and support one another in this journey.

5b792818-ac34-4256-97f8-cdce43bde7f0_rev.product_description: Reflection on career journey and transitions in employment

actual_review_data.review_text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe! Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe

actual_review_data.review_text:
I'm happy to present to open-source enthusiasts and computer science students about the intricacies of OpenTelemetry and how it can be leveraged to build efficient and scalable systems.

I'll also showcase how telemetry data can be fed into a data visualization tool like Grafana Labs' Grafana with the help of the `grafana-lgtm` docker container stack.

Thank you for this opportunity, Răzvan Deaconescu and ROSEdu.
Thank you to everyone who contributes to the open-source space.
actual_review_data.product_description: Open-source technologies and their application in software development

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As a software engineer at Microsoft, I’ve seen firsthand the transformative power of open-source technologies in software development. These tools not only enhance our capabilities but also democratize access to advanced solutions for developers at all levels. 

Open-source projects foster collaboration and innovation, allowing us to leverage community-driven insights and improvements. Whether it’s utilizing frameworks like React or integrating with tools like OpenTelemetry for observability, the impact is profound.

actual_review_data.review_text:
This post is dedicated to my past employer, Ubisoft, and to my time spent working as a QA/QC engineer for the `Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora` video game.

I am thrilled to witness the release of the game and the positive reviews that have come along. Avatar is a vast franchise, spanning many fields of creativity.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

#Ubisoft #Avatar
actual_review_data.product_description: Career experiences in the video game industry

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Reflecting on my journey in the video game industry, I’m reminded of the invaluable lessons learned that have shaped my career as a software engineer at Microsoft. Working at Ubisoft, I had the privilege of collaborating with talented teams, tackling complex challenges, and witnessing firsthand the power of creativity and technology in gaming. 

One key takeaway from my time in game development is the importance of resilience. The iterative nature of game design taught me that failure is not the end but a stepping stone to success. Each bug fixed and feature polished was a reminder that perseverance pays off. 

Moreover, mentorship played a crucial role in my growth. I was fortunate to have mentors who guided me through the intricacies of game mechanics and coding practices. Now, I strive to pay it forward by mentoring aspiring developers, sharing insights, and fostering a supportive community. 

As we navigate our careers, let’s remember to embrace our experiences, learn from our challenges, and uplift one another. The skills we acquire in one industry can often translate into unexpected strengths in another. Let’s keep pushing boundaries and supporting each other on this journey! #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship #GameDevelopment #Resilience

actual_review_data.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.
actual_review_data.product_description: Professional development and learning strategies

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
In today's fast-paced tech landscape, continuous professional development is not just a luxury—it's a necessity. As someone who has navigated the challenges of career growth in software engineering, I've learned that effective learning strategies can make all the difference. Here are a few actionable tips that have worked for me and many of my peers:

1. **Set Clear Goals**: Define what you want to achieve in your career. Whether it's mastering a new technology, transitioning to a leadership role, or enhancing your soft skills, having a clear vision helps you stay focused.

2. **Leverage Online Resources**: Platforms like Coursera, Udacity, and even GitHub offer a wealth of knowledge. Don't hesitate to dive into tutorials, courses, or open-source projects that align with your goals.

3. **Engage with the Community**: Networking is crucial. Join tech meetups, webinars, or online forums. Sharing experiences and learning from others can provide insights that you won't find in textbooks.

4. **Seek Mentorship**: Finding a mentor can be a game-changer. They can provide guidance, support, and valuable feedback as you navigate your career path.

5. **Reflect and Adapt**: Regularly assess your progress and be open to adjusting your strategies. The tech industry evolves quickly, and so should your approach to learning.

Remember, professional development is a journey, not a destination. Let's support each other in our growth and celebrate our milestones together! What strategies have you found effective in your career development? Share your thoughts below! #CareerDevelopment #LearningStrategies #Mentorship #CommunitySupport

5. **Celebrate Milestones Together**: Acknowledge your achievements and those of your peers. Let's support each other in our growth, recognizing the emotional experiences we share along the way.

actual_review_data.review_text: HDFC Bank Your representatives call multiple times a week asking me if I want to increase my credit limit . If I tell them to not call me again about it , they just ignore it. Called me twice today about it. Why are you pestering people about it? Might as well get rid of HDFC credit cards & account due to the insane amount of marketing calls. None of them seem to be able to mark a customer as "not interested" and not call again. Really disappointed with you. I will also complain to banking ombudsman and to Dept of Telecom (DOT) for misusing our contact number to spam us with marketing calls. Shameful

Our Ignite session where I talk about upcoming capabilities of SharePoint Syntex and how Microsoft uses AI for content processing & generation at scale. 
Super exciting times for our team.

Nitin Sharma Ashutosh Tripathi Ravi Gopinath Sudha Narayanan Harsh Garg Namrata Jha
#Microsoft #SharePoint #contentservices #ai #machinelearnig #microsoftignite
actual_review_data.product_description: Utilization of AI technologies in content processing

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we continue to explore the integration of AI technologies in content processing, I'm excited to share some insights on how these advancements are transforming our workflows at Microsoft. By leveraging tools like SharePoint Syntex, we can automate content classification and enhance document understanding, ultimately freeing up valuable time for our teams to focus on strategic initiatives. 

One of the key benefits I've observed is the increased accessibility of these technologies. With user-friendly interfaces and robust documentation, even those without a deep technical background can harness the power of AI to improve their content management processes. This democratization of technology is crucial as we strive for inclusivity and efficiency in our organizations.

I encourage everyone to explore the practical applications of AI in your own workflows. Whether it's automating routine tasks or gaining deeper insights from your data, the potential is immense. Let's continue to share our experiences and learn from each other as we navigate this exciting landscape together. 

#AI #ContentProcessing #SharePointSyntex #Microsoft #Innovation #CommunitySupport

actual_review_data.review_text: One of my good friends way back from college - Shivakumar Kn is looking out for new opportunities. He has more than 20 years experience in software development field, and has till recently worked as Application Development manager/solution architect. Please contact him if you have any openings which match his profile.
actual_review_data.product_description: Opportunities and job searching in the tech industry

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating the tech job market can feel daunting, especially with the rapid changes in our industry. As someone who has been on both sides of the hiring table, I want to share some insights that might help you in your job search. 

1. **Leverage Your Network**: Don’t underestimate the power of connections. Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, and even classmates. They can often provide leads or referrals that are not advertised.

2. **Showcase Your Skills**: In a competitive market, having a strong portfolio or GitHub profile can set you apart. Share your projects, contributions to open-source, or any relevant work that demonstrates your expertise.

3. **Stay Updated**: The tech landscape is always evolving. Make it a habit to learn about new tools and technologies, especially those relevant to your field. Certifications can also add value to your resume.

4. **Be Open to Opportunities**: Sometimes the right role might not be what you initially envisioned. Be open to exploring positions that may stretch your skills or take you into new areas of interest.

5. **Prioritize Mental Health**: Job searching can be stressful. Remember to take breaks, seek support from your community, and practice self-care. Resilience is key during this journey.

Lastly, if you're looking for opportunities or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey. Together, we can navigate the challenges and celebrate the wins! #CareerDevelopment #JobSearch #TechIndustry #CommunitySupport

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8_rev.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

actual_review_data.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.
actual_review_data.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As someone who has navigated the complexities of pursuing higher education in the United States, I understand how overwhelming the process can be. From selecting the right program to understanding the nuances of international student requirements, it’s crucial to equip yourself with the right information and resources. 

Here are a few key pieces of advice I wish I had known earlier:

1. **Research Thoroughly**: Take the time to explore various institutions and their programs. Look beyond rankings; consider factors like faculty expertise, campus culture, and support services for international students.

2. **Connect with Alumni**: Reach out to alumni from your desired programs. They can provide invaluable insights into their experiences and offer guidance on what to expect.

3. **Understand Financial Aid Options**: Many institutions offer scholarships and financial aid specifically for international students. Make sure to investigate these opportunities early in your application process.

4. **Prepare for Cultural Adjustments**: Moving to a new country comes with its own set of challenges. Be open to learning and adapting to different cultural norms, and don’t hesitate to seek support from campus resources.

5. **Leverage Networking Opportunities**: Attend workshops, seminars, and events related to your field of study. Networking can open doors to internships and job opportunities that are crucial for your career.

Remember, pursuing higher education is not just about academics; it’s about personal growth and building a future. If you have any questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship

Navigating the complexities of pursuing higher education in the United States can be overwhelming, especially for international students. Drawing from my own experiences, I want to share some key insights that can help ease this journey, particularly in light of the current challenges we face.

1. **Research Thoroughly**: Explore various institutions and their programs. Look beyond rankings; consider factors like faculty expertise, campus culture, and support services for international students. This can significantly impact your experience and success.

2. **Connect with Alumni**: Reach out to alumni from your desired programs. Their insights can be invaluable, providing guidance on what to expect and how to navigate the system effectively.

3. **Understand Financial Aid Options**: Many institutions offer scholarships and financial aid specifically for international students. Investigate these opportunities early in your application process to alleviate some financial burdens.

4. **Prepare for Cultural Adjustments**: Moving to a new country comes with challenges. Be open to learning and adapting to different cultural norms. Utilize campus resources for support; you're not alone in this transition.

5. **Leverage Networking Opportunities**: Attend workshops, seminars, and events related to your field of study. Networking can lead to internships and job opportunities, which are crucial for your career, especially given the current visa restrictions impacting job prospects.

Remember, pursuing higher education is not just about academics; it’s about personal growth and building a future. If you have any questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship #Resilience

ab1fb966-d14e-4639-b504-00f7b6f9084b_rev.product_description: Exploration of personal challenges impacting careers

actual_review_data.review_text:
Estatisticas mundias estimam que "aprox. 70% das pessoas irão vivenciar sindrome de impostor pelo menos uma vez na vida" ( Essa experiência afeta principalmente as mulheres e, caso não identificada e persistente, pode ter impacto considerável em nossa carreira. 
A comunidade Brasil do The WIT Network faz um convite a você para participar de um bate papo online sobre o tema. Venha conhecer mais sobre a Sindrome de Impostor, bem como ouvir as estorias e dicas de profissionais talentosos que vivenciaram e superaram essa experiencia. 
Data: 16 de Outubro, às 19h BRT. 

[English]
World statistics estimate that "around 70% of people in the world will experience Imposter Syndrome at least once in their lives"( It is likely to affect women. 
Non identified cases or persistently experiencing Imposter Syndrome may affect your career progression. With this in mind, the Brazilian Community of The WIT Network invites you to join an online roundtable about the subject. Join us to know more about Imposter Syndrome and hear the amazing stories from talented professionals that overcame it.
Date: October 16th at 7PM BRT (GMT-3) 
Note: the event will be held in Portuguese.

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating personal challenges can often feel like an uphill battle, especially when it comes to our careers. I've faced my share of obstacles, from self-doubt to balancing work with personal responsibilities. It's a journey that many of us can relate to, and I believe it's crucial to share these experiences. 

In the tech industry, where innovation and performance are highly valued, we sometimes forget the emotional toll that our careers can take. It’s important to acknowledge that mental health plays a significant role in our professional lives. Resilience and adaptability are not just buzzwords; they are essential skills that we must cultivate. 

I encourage everyone in our community to open up about their challenges. Let's foster a culture where we can support one another through mentorship and shared experiences. Remember, it's okay to ask for help and to lean on your network. Together, we can create a more inclusive environment that values both technical skills and emotional well-being. 

What challenges have you faced in your career, and how have you navigated them? Let's share our stories and support each other on this journey.

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we continue to explore the transformative power of AI in education, I am excited to share some insights on how we can leverage these technologies to enhance skills development. At Microsoft, we are witnessing firsthand how AI applications can create personalized learning experiences that cater to individual needs, making education more accessible and effective. 

One of the most promising areas is the integration of AI-driven platforms that analyze student performance in real-time, allowing educators to tailor their teaching strategies accordingly. This not only fosters a more engaging learning environment but also empowers students to take charge of their own learning journeys. 

Moreover, as we embrace no-code solutions, we can democratize access to these tools, enabling educators and students alike to harness the power of AI without needing extensive technical expertise. This shift not only enhances educational outcomes but also prepares our future workforce for the demands of a rapidly evolving job market. 

I encourage all professionals in our community to share their experiences and insights on implementing AI in education. Together, we can build a more inclusive and innovative ecosystem that supports lifelong learning and skills development. Let's continue to inspire and empower each other in this journey! #AI #Education #SkillsDevelopment #Microsoft #CommunitySupport

In the last week I decided to close a cycle in my life. I finished a fantastic journey of 15 years working on research and development at Eldorado. 
Thank you Eldorado friends for all support and great work that has changed so many customers' business, that has contributed to show that it is possible to do innovation and research in Brazil.
Thank you also to all partners and customers that gave me the chance to learn with you.
Now, it is time to start a new journey full of new innovation challenges. 
 Let's empower every people to innovate.
#newjourney #gratefulness #innovation

actual_review_data.review_text:
If you are interested in Data Engineering and you are based on LATAM, please read this!

At Factored we are on a mission to make a positive contribution to LATAM. The way we want to do this is by building high-caliber data teams ready to show the world the talent we have in Latin America.

Last year I embarked on a mission to help people understand more about Data Engineering. I had the opportunity to talk to almost 30 people about data engineering and share some insights about the profession with them.

I've decided again to open some slots on my schedule this year! :)
So, if you are into Data Engineering and you are interested to join Factored or you want to get some guidance on Data Engineering, please reach out to me. Also, if you know anyone interested in this topic, please tag them so we can get in touch! :)

You can find the link to grab a slot on my calendar in the comments :)
#factored #dataengineering #data #latinamerica
